expectations.text: Uses Zod or equivalent schema for response parsing
expectations.evidence: Lines 7-12: UserProfileSchema = z.object({ name: z.string(), email: z.string(), role: z.enum(userRoles), phoneNumber: z.string().optional() })

expectations.text: Type derived from schema (z.infer or equivalent), not a hand-written interface
expectations.evidence: Line 14: export type UserProfile = z.infer<typeof UserProfileSchema>. Type derives from schema, not the reverse.

expectations.text: Parsing happens at the API boundary function, not deferred to consumers
expectations.evidence: Line 18: return UserProfileSchema.parse(response.data). Parsing at the boundary. Consumers receive fully typed UserProfile.

expectations.text: Uses named export, not default export
expectations.evidence: Line 14: export type UserProfile; Line 16: export async function fetchUserProfile. Both named exports.
